nuggets.headline: Before music, Jamee Cornelia filmed her skate team because she wasn't the best skater.
nuggets.text: Jamee Cornelia’s journey into the creative world started young, at just 15 years old in Fuquay Varina, North Carolina. She launched a clothing company to sponsor her skate team, then picked up a camera to film them because she had access to free equipment through AFJROTC and wasn't the strongest skater herself. This early pivot led her to become the 'go-to music video director' in the Atlanta area after Odd Future's rise, setting the stage for her diverse career in music as a solo artist and former lead vocalist for the punk band Howling Star.
nuggets.imageCaption: Jamee Cornelia: artist, director, label co-founder, punk vocalist.

nuggets.headline: Your current listen, 'SLACK', is from Jamee Cornelia's recent album 'HARVEST'.
nuggets.text: You're hearing 'SLACK', a standout track from Jamee Cornelia’s newest album, 'HARVEST', which dropped on February 6, 2026. This album continues Jamee's reputation for diverse musical output, following releases like 'ART SCHOOL DROPOUT' and 'Big Homie'. It’s a testament to her versatility that she moves seamlessly between her solo work and her past as a punk band vocalist with Howling Star, showcasing a wide range of influences in her sound.

nuggets.headline: If 'SLACK' hits right, you'll love the intricate production of Namir Blade.
nuggets.text: If you're digging the sound of Jamee Cornelia's 'SLACK', then you absolutely need to check out Namir Blade next. Namir Blade, a phenomenal artist, often weaves together intricate, sometimes atmospheric, and deeply thoughtful production with a smooth yet impactful vocal delivery. His style has a similar genre-bending quality that explores hip-hop with experimental and alternative textures, much like Jamee’s own diverse approach. Give his album 'Aphelion' a spin; you won't regret it.
nuggets.imageCaption: Namir Blade, whose unique sound might just be your next listen.

nuggets.headline: Facing challenges as a Queer Black Woman in the South led Jamee Cornelia to 'travel OFTEN'
nuggets.text: Jamee Cornelia identifies as a Queer Black Woman, and she's been open about facing significant challenges and not being taken seriously in the male-dominated creative scene of the South, particularly in Cobb County and her North Carolina hometown. She's found a place to truly thrive in East Atlanta, and her powerful advice to anyone facing similar hurdles is to 'travel OFTEN,' believing in the strength and self-worth found in exploring new environments.
nuggets.imageCaption: Jamee Cornelia, a Queer Black Woman artist from North Carolina.

nuggets.headline: The name 'L7 Records' connects Jamee Cornelia's latest album, 'HARVEST', to her high school dreams
nuggets.text: The album 'HARVEST', which features 'SLACK', is Jamee Cornelia's latest full-length release, showcasing 13 new songs. It arrives courtesy of L7 Records, the indie label she co-runs with friends from her Visibly Inflight collective. The label's name itself is a nod to her roots, stemming from a clothing store she dreamed of opening in high school for her very first creative venture: a clothing company.

nuggets.headline: For another artist with laid-back grooves and clever bars, check out Nappy Nina
nuggets.text: If the smooth flow and intricate production of Jamee Cornelia on 'SLACK' have you hooked, you absolutely need to dive into the world of Nappy Nina. Her music often features a similar unhurried, yet deeply engaging vocal delivery over beats that are both fresh and sometimes wonderfully off-kilter. You'll find yourself appreciating the thoughtful lyricism and chill vibes, just like with Jamee's work.
nuggets.imageCaption: Nappy Nina, an artist sharing sonic qualities with Jamee Cornelia.

nuggets.headline: Before solo albums, Jamee Cornelia fronted the punk band Howling Star.
nuggets.text: Long before her solo music as a rapper and producer, Jamee Cornelia was the lead vocalist for a punk band called Howling Star for four years. During that time, she also co-founded Punk Black, a concert series dedicated to celebrating alternative Black artists and their scenes.
nuggets.imageCaption: Jamee Cornelia's diverse creative journey, from punk to hip-hop.

nuggets.headline: 'HARVEST' marks a new phase in Jamee Cornelia's evolving journey.
nuggets.text: Your current listen, "SLACK," is from Jamee Cornelia's latest album, "HARVEST," which dropped on February 6, 2026. This 13-song release feels like a culmination, reflecting her extensive creative path from a teen clothing company owner to a seasoned rapper, producer, and filmmaker.

nuggets.headline: For more compelling flows like 'SLACK,' check out rapper Yani Mo.
nuggets.text: If "SLACK" is hitting you with its confident delivery and intricate production, you'll definitely want to dive into Yani Mo's catalog. Like Jamee Cornelia, Yani Mo brings a compelling lyrical style and a unique atmospheric quality to her hip-hop, making for a smooth yet engaging listen that feels both modern and thoughtful.
nuggets.imageCaption: Yani Mo, another artist with a compelling lyrical flow.
